title: Tragacantha prolixa

The genus Tragacantha is often associated with the subfamily Faboideae, which includes a wide range of legume species. At the species level, Tragacantha prolixa is further classified within its own distinct taxonomic group. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and current taxonomic understanding.

intro:
I couldn't find much information on the plant "Tragacantha prolixa". However, it seems to be a synonym for the plant "Astragalus prolixa" or possibly a different species,  "Astragalus" is a genus of plant that is often referred to as "Milkvetch" or "Tragacanth". 

Astragalus prolixa, also known as "Milkvetch", is a plant that is used in traditional medicine. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus prolixa include flavonoids, saponins, and polysaccharides, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. Astragalus prolixa is often used to make herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
